report_by_pk.title: AI Template Engine Penetration Test (2026-02-13) Report

findings.title: HSTS
findings.description: The application does not enforce HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS). This allows attackers to perform man-in-the-middle attacks by stripping TLS from connections.
findings.impact: An attacker on the same network could intercept sensitive data transmitted between the user and the application.
findings.mitigation: Configure the web server to include the Strict-Transport-Security header with an appropriate max-age value.
findings.replication_steps:
1. Navigate to the application using HTTP
2. Observe that no redirect to HTTPS occurs
3. Check response headers for missing HSTS header

findings.title: mass form
findings.description: Mass assignment vulnerability found in form submission endpoint. Attacker can modify fields not intended for user input.
findings.impact: Limited impact due to server-side validation, but could allow privilege escalation in certain conditions.
findings.mitigation: Implement strict allowlists for form parameters. Reject any unexpected fields.
findings.replication_steps:
1. Intercept form submission request
2. Add role=admin parameter
3. Observe server processes the extra parameter

findings.title: SQLI
findings.description: SQL injection vulnerability discovered in the search parameter. The application concatenates user input directly into SQL queries without parameterisation.
findings.impact: An attacker could extract all data from the database, including user credentials and sensitive business data.
findings.mitigation: Use parameterised queries or prepared statements for all database interactions. Implement input validation as a defence-in-depth measure.
findings.replication_steps:
1. Navigate to search functionality
2. Enter payload: ' OR 1=1 --
3. Observe that all records are returned

findings.title: XSS Reflected
findings.description: Reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability found in the search results page. User input is reflected in the page without proper encoding.
findings.impact: An attacker could execute arbitrary JavaScript in the context of a victim's browser session, potentially stealing session tokens or performing actions on their behalf.
findings.mitigation: Encode all user-controlled output using context-appropriate encoding. Implement Content Security Policy headers.
findings.replication_steps:
1. Navigate to search page
2. Enter payload: <script>alert(1)</script>
3. Observe JavaScript execution in browser
